title: Diverse Dataset on Same Task
description: Keep in Survey: the same-task HOI dataset is still actionable even with completed setup tasks; the next decision is whether the unified object/contact schema produces useful cross-embodiment supervision.
summary: This pre-research project asks whether the same manipulation task can be collected across all major acquisition modes, then unified into an HOI-centric base model and predictive world model.
asset_alt: HOI dataset capture frames with hand pose and depth map
asset_caption: Current capture stack: ego RGB, hand keypoints, z16 depth, and shared tracking logic.
details: Collection modes: human-centric and robot-centric views; Manus glove, UMI, ALOHA, kinesthetic / drag demonstration, and teleoperation.

Reference framing: HOI-consistent retargeting, not joint-angle imitation. The target is that the human hand and robot hand manipulate the same object into the same HOI outcome: matched object pose / velocity trajectory, matched contact intent, matched grasp or tool-use mode, and matched task result.

Retargeting pipeline: capture glove / human posture plus object pose, lift them into a shared 3D object frame, infer contact patches / normals / timing, propose robot wrist and hand configurations, then optimize for object-state transition and contact consistency rather than visual hand-pose similarity.

Isaac Sim bridge: replay candidate robot trajectories on the same USD object and scene, drive the robot through articulation commands, read contact / collision / object-pose results, and reject candidates that look plausible kinematically but slide, penetrate, drop the object, violate joint limits, or fail to reproduce the human HOI result.
Recent status: 2077AI spent substantial time on sensor synchronization; camera extrinsics now have an extra PnP calibration layer, and hand pose, depth, and ego camera are mostly ready.
risks_decisions: Data value depends on same-task equivalence across embodiments, not only collecting more modalities.
2077AI collection capacity and sensor-sync reliability are current bottlenecks; the pipeline needs simple validation checks before scaling.

Human pose maps to multiple feasible robot poses, so retargeting labels should be object-centric: equivalent object trajectory, contact sequence, and task success are more important than matching human finger geometry.
Object and hand tracking should share calibration assumptions; otherwise object-state supervision will drift from hand pose and depth.
Predictive WM scope should start with aligned HOI state transitions before attempting a full cross-embodiment policy model.
Isaac validation depends on usable object assets: collision mesh, mass, friction, joint limits, and contact sensing must be good enough before treating simulation pass / fail as ground truth.

Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because no cited paper has completed our exact goal of collecting one manipulation task across human, glove, UMI, ALOHA, drag-teach, and teleop modes under a shared object/contact schema.
Next executable analysis: pick one task, freeze the object/contact annotation pack, and decide whether Isaac retargeting pass/fail is reliable enough to be a dataset quality gate.
Do not archive just because current TODOs are done; this card is the survey holder for the next cross-modality capture decision.
